risk_name: EPC/EPCM Contractor Performance Failure — Schedule, Quality, or Safety

description.summary: The EPC or EPCM contractor fails to meet contractual schedule, quality, or safety standards. Hydrogen projects face a specific challenge: limited number of EPC contractors with demonstrated large-scale electrolysis experience. The Gold Dataset shows projects using diverse EPC models (EPCM, split contracts, owner's engineer) — reflecting the immaturity of the hydrogen EPC market.
description.detailed: The hydrogen EPC market is immature. Traditional EPC contractors (Technip, Worley, Linde Engineering, Technicas Reunidas) have transferable skills from oil & gas and chemical industries but limited electrolysis-specific track records. The Gold Dataset shows varied approaches: HH1 uses Worley as EPCM (engineering, procurement, construction management — owner retains more risk), HGHH split the scope (Siemens Energy for electrolyzer, Kraftanlagen for BOP), HyDeal España engaged 4 separate FEED contractors. This fragmentation increases integration risk but reflects the reality that no single EPC contractor has delivered a >100 MW green hydrogen plant.
description.root_cause: Immature hydrogen EPC market. Limited number of contractors with demonstrated electrolysis experience at >20 MW scale. Transferable skills from oil & gas and chemicals exist but electrolysis-specific integration expertise is developing.
trigger_events: EPC contractor reports schedule variance >10% from baseline
EPC contractor experiences key personnel turnover on project
Subcontractor default or insolvency affecting critical path
EPC contractor requests significant change order outside contingency

assessment.probability_rationale: First-of-a-kind projects with contractors lacking electrolysis-specific track records carry elevated EPC performance risk. As the market matures (2028+), this probability should decrease.
assessment.impact_rationale: EPC performance failure can delay COD by 6-12 months and add 10-25% to construction cost through change orders and claims.
assessment.detectability_rationale: Monthly EPC progress reports provide partial visibility. But true performance issues (productivity, quality defects) may not be apparent until 30-50% through construction.
assessment.assessment_method: Industrial project benchmarking + Gold Dataset EPC model analysis

preventive_actions.description: Use competitive FEED + EPC procurement with minimum 3 qualified bidders. Require demonstration of electrolysis or chlor-alkali project experience.
Engage Owner's Engineer with hydrogen project experience to provide independent oversight of EPC contractor performance. TSK serves this role on HyDeal España.

incident_descriptions: Holland Hydrogen I (GA-PR-003): Worley as EPCM. Shell retained significant owner oversight — reflecting Shell's in-house engineering capability and the immaturity of the hydrogen EPC market.

Hamburg Green Hydrogen Hub (GA-PR-004): Split scope approach: Siemens Energy for electrolyzer (OEM), Kraftanlagen for BOP, Drees & Sommer for project management. This split model manages risk by matching each scope to the most qualified contractor but increases integration complexity.
HyDeal España (GA-PR-005): 4 FEED contractors (VINCI/Cobra, Técnicas Reunidas, PowerChina, TSK as Owner's Engineer). Competitive FEED approach to de-risk technology and cost before EPC selection.
Masshylia (GA-PR-002): No EPC contractor appointed — project has not reached that stage, illustrating that EPC market readiness is contingent on project maturity.

sources.title: Hydrogen Council / McKinsey — Hydrogen Insights 2024: EPC market analysis
sources.author: Hydrogen Council
